steps.title: Identify the clause boundary
steps.content: The sentence joins two complete, parallel descriptions of Sokol's prints: sweatpants become a camel, and pliers become flip-flops. The blank must separate those two independent clauses correctly.

steps.title: Reject weak separators
steps.content: Choice A creates a comma splice between independent clauses. Choice C suggests an interruption or aside rather than a standard clause join. Choice D uses a colon even though the second clause is a parallel example, not an introduced list.

steps.title: Use the semicolon
steps.content: Choice B supplies a semicolon after clothesline, correctly linking the two closely related independent clauses about Sokol's surreal household images. The answer is B.
